qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Multipart continuation undercounts part sizes and bypasses upload limits
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding targets multipart quota accounting in `wisp.require_form` and `multipart_body`, while the advisory targets percent-encoded traversal in `serve_static` caused by sanitizing `..` before `uri.percent_decode`.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: No final qualifying finding identifies the advisory's `serve_static` root cause: percent-encoded `..` survives sanitization because decoding happens after `string.replace(each: "..", with: "")`, enabling arbitrary file read.
evidence: The advisory and upstream fix both center on `serve_static` in `src/wisp.gleam`, where the patch moves `uri.percent_decode` ahead of `string.replace(each: "..", with: "")` to block `%2e%2e` traversal.

The only manifest-listed final qualifying finding is `multipart_quota_bypass`, whose report and reproduced PoC concern `wisp.require_form` multipart continuation handling in `multipart_body` around `src/wisp.gleam:1082-1172`.
That multipart finding demonstrates oversized upload acceptance and availability impact, not traversal to `serve_static`, percent-decoding behavior, or arbitrary file read.
